fields.objective: Do the task and publish the completion event when finished.

fields.prompt:
You are inside a bare-minimal miniloops harness.

Objective:
Do the task and publish the completion event when finished.


Iteration: 1/3
Completion event: task.complete
Completion promise fallback: LOOP_COMPLETE
Required events: (none)
Suggested next roles now: (none)
Allowed next events now: (none)
Event tool: ./.miniloops/miniloops

Current scratchpad:
(empty)

Use the event tool to publish progress and completion.
Examples:
./.miniloops/miniloops emit task.progress "brief update"
./.miniloops/miniloops emit task.complete "brief completion summary"
./.miniloops/miniloops memory add learning "durable lesson"
./.miniloops/miniloops memory add preference Workflow "short preference note"

Backpressure rule: if you emit an event outside the allowed next-event set, the loop will reject that handoff and ask you to re-route.
Prompt/output/scratchpad/memory are projections or stores you can inspect with `./.miniloops/miniloops inspect prompt 1 --format md`, `./.miniloops/miniloops inspect scratchpad --format md`, and `./.miniloops/miniloops inspect memory --format md`.
Plain text alone does not publish an event. Prefer the event tool over the stdout completion promise.

fields.output: sh: ./examples/mock-backend.sh: No such file or directory
